title: Figure Generator
purpose: Create publication-quality charts from CSV or Excel datasets with scientific formatting and vector export.
learningTime: Learn this tool in under 2 minutes
capabilities: Bar, line, scatter, and box plots

tour.title: Load data
tour.body: Drop or select a CSV or Excel file. Preview parsed columns before charting.

tour.title: Chart configuration
tour.body: Select chart type, map columns to axes, and adjust titles and styling.

tour.title: Live preview
tour.body: Review the figure with lab-standard typography and colors before export.

tour.title: Export
tour.body: Download PNG for slides, SVG for Illustrator, or PDF for manuscripts.

overview.purpose: Transform tabular experimental data into formatted figures suitable for lab meetings and publications.
overview.useCase: Plot qPCR fold changes, growth curves, or assay readouts without manual Illustrator work.
overview.inputs: CSV or Excel files with numeric columns and optional grouping variables.
overview.outputs: PNG, SVG, or PDF figure files.

workflow.title: Upload data
workflow.description: Load your spreadsheet. The preview panel shows detected columns.

workflow.title: Select chart type
workflow.description: Choose bar, line, scatter, or box plot based on your data structure.

workflow.title: Configure
workflow.description: Assign X/Y columns, set axis labels, and adjust dimensions.

tips: Use clear column headers — they become axis labels by default.
Prefer SVG or PDF for journal submissions requiring vector graphics.
Keep color palettes consistent with your lab's other figures.

faq.a: Standard comma-separated values. Excel files are parsed with the first sheet used by default.

faq.q: Can I edit the chart after export?
faq.a: SVG exports can be opened in Illustrator or Inkscape for fine-tuning.

items: Initial release with bar, line, scatter, and box plots
